metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5226
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 5: 14DIV primary rat hippocampal cells stained for SEPTIN2 and SEPTIN5 showed the former is present in glia and neurons, while the latter is only expressed in neurons (also shown by western blot in Fig. 3). SEPTIN2 and SEPTIN5 only co-localized to a subset of neurons.

Figure 6: in biochemical fractions prepared from adult rat brains, SEPTIN2 was detected with constant expression levels over all fractions including synaptic fractions P2, synaptosomes, synaptosomal membranes, soluble synaptosomal content and crude synaptic vesicle fraction.. Experimental description: Results, Figure 6 legend: "Proteins in various membrane fractions prepared from adult rat brain were probed for septins with the indicated a-septin antibodies."

antibody:
The manuscript mentions "SEPT2 and SEPT4 antibodies were described previously (Xie et al., 1999)", but this manuscript doesn't seem to describe the antibody. Regardless, the SEPT2 antibody used in the paper annotated here was validated using a LexA-fusion-protein (Figure 1).
